ground_truth.title: 51 Must-Have Electrician Tools for 2025
ground_truth.author: Tom Talbot
ground_truth.main_content:
Electricians like the ones in your business rely on more than just skills, and having the right electrician tools can make or break a project. The right electrician tools ensure your team can work safely, efficiently and profitably.

In this guide, we’ve organized 51 must-have electrician tools for 2025 into categories so you can quickly see what belongs in your kit. Plus, we’ll share one bonus tool that doesn’t fit in your toolbox—but can transform your electrical business.

Basic Hand Tools Every Electrician Needs

1. Multimeter
A multimeter is the backbone of every electrician’s toolkit. It measures voltage, current and resistance, allowing you to troubleshoot electrical problems accurately. 

Choose a high-quality digital multimeter with auto-ranging capabilities for ease of use.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Fluke 117
Klein Tools MM400

2. Wire Strippers
Wire strippers are one of the most indispensable, must-have electrician tools for removing the insulation from electrical wires. Look for a pair with adjustable cutting slots to accommodate different wire gauges. A good pair of wire strippers will save you time and effort while ensuring clean and precise wire stripping.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Klein Tools 11061
IRWIN VISE-GRIP

3. Needle-Nose Pliers:
Needle-nose pliers are one of the most versatile, must-have electrician tools that come in handy for various electrical tasks. They have long, tapered jaws that allow you to:
Bend wires 
Reach into tight spaces
Grip small components 

Opt for pliers with comfortable handles and a solid construction for durability.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
IRWIN VISE-GRIP Long Nose Pliers 
Channellock 317 Long Nose Pliers 

4. Lineman’s Pliers
Lineman’s pliers, also known as combination pliers, are one of the top robust, must-have electrician tools designed for cutting, twisting and gripping wires. Plus, they have a built-in crimper for making wire connections and cutting blades for heavy-duty wire cutting. Choose pliers with comfortable handles and a hardened cutting edge.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Knipex Tools 09 02 240 Lineman’s Pliers 
Southwire Tools 58993940

5. Screwdrivers
A set of high-quality screwdrivers is essential for any electrician. You’ll need both flathead and Phillips-head screwdrivers in different sizes to handle various electrical equipment, outlets and switches. Look for screwdrivers with insulated handles to provide protection against electric shock.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Klein Tools 85078 Set 
Craftsman 931794 Set 

6. Utility Knife
A sharp utility knife is versatile for cutting various materials including wire insulation, cable sheathing and packaging. Look for one with a retractable blade for safety and easy blade replacement.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools Utility Knife
Stanley Retractable

7. Headlamp or Flashlight
Good lighting is essential for electrical work, especially in dark spaces like basements, attics and electrical panels. A hands-free headlamp allows you to work while maintaining illumination on your workspace.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Petzl Tikka Headlamp
Streamlight MicroStream LED Flashlight

8. Measuring Tape
A durable measuring tape is essential for calculating wire lengths, spacing electrical components and planning installations. Choose one with clear markings and a sturdy case.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Stanley FatMax 25-Foot Measuring Tape
Klein Tools 25-Foot Measuring Tape

9. Cable Cutters
For cutting larger cables and conductors, a pair of cable cutters is essential. These heavy-duty cutters are designed to cleanly cut through thick cables without distorting or damaging them. Look for cable cutters with hardened steel blades and ergonomic handles for ease of use.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Klein Tools 63050 Cable Cutters
DEWALT DCE150B Cable Cutting Tool

10. Portable Work Light
High-quality portable work lights provide adequate illumination for electrical work in dark or poorly lit areas.

Top Expert Recommendations:
DEWALT 20V LED Work Light
Milwaukee M18 LED Flood Light

Pro tip! Just like the right hand tools save you time on the jobsite, the best electrician software saves you hours of paperwork by streamlining scheduling and invoicing.

Testing and Measuring Tools

11. Circuit Tester
A circuit tester is a small, handheld device that helps you determine whether an electrical circuit is live or not. It is an essential safety tool to ensure you’re working on de-energized circuits. Invest in a high-quality voltage tester with a reliable indicator and built-in safety features.

Top Expert Recommendations 
Sperry Instruments STK001 Non-Contact Voltage 
Gardner Bender GVD-3504 Circuit Alert Voltage 

12. Non-Contact Voltage Tester
A non-contact voltage tester enables you to detect live wires without making direct contact. It uses electromagnetic field detection to alert you to the presence of voltage. This is one of the must-have electrician tools that’s handy for quick and safe voltage checks before working on electrical systems.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Fluke VoltAlert
Fluke T5-1000

13. Outlet Tester
An outlet tester quickly identifies wiring problems in electrical outlets, including open grounds, reversed polarity and other common issues. This diagnostic tool saves time during troubleshooting.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools RT110 Outlet Tester
Klein Tools RT210 GFCI Outlet Tester

14. Digital Clamp Meter
Digital clamp meters measure current flow without breaking the circuit by clamping around conductors, making them essential for troubleshooting.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke 323
Fluke 325

15. Thermal Imaging Camera
Thermal imaging cameras detect heat patterns in electrical equipment, helping identify overloaded circuits, loose connections and other potential problems.

Top Expert Recommendations:
FLIR TG267 Spot Thermal Camera
FLIR TG165-X Thermal Imaging Camera

16. Oscilloscope and Power Quality Analyzer
Oscilloscopes display electrical waveforms, allowing electricians to analyze complex electrical signals and troubleshoot sophisticated electronic systems.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Rigol DS1054Z Digital Oscilloscope
Hantek DSO2C10 Digital Oscilloscope

17. Electrical Tester with FieldSense
Advanced electrical testers with FieldSense technology allow you to measure voltage and current without making contact with live wires, providing enhanced safety during electrical work.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke T6-1000 Electrical Tester
Fluke T6-1000 PRO Electrical Tester

18. Insulation Tester (Megohmmeter)
An insulation tester measures the electrical resistance of insulation in cables and equipment, helping identify potential failures before they occur.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke 1507 Insulation Resistance Tester
Megger MIT400 Series Insulation Tester

Safety Gear and PPE

19. Insulated Gloves and Safety Gear:
Personal safety should always be a top priority for electricians. Also, it’s a requirement. Insulated gloves provide protection against electric shock while working with live wires. Additionally, wearing safety glasses, a hard hat and protective clothing ensures you’re safeguarded against potential hazards.

Top Expert Recommendations: 
Magid Safety Electrical Gloves 
Salisbury Honeywell E011B Insulated Electrical Gloves

20. Arc Flash PPE
Arc flash personal protective equipment protects electricians from the thermal energy released during electrical arc faults.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Oberon Arc Flash Suits
Enespro Arc Flash Protection

21. Safety Lockout/Tagout Kit
Lockout/tagout kits ensure electrical equipment remains safely de-energized during maintenance work by preventing accidental re-energization.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Master Lock Electrical Lockout Kit
Brady Electrical Lockout Tagout Kit

22. Voltage Detector Pen
Non-contact voltage detector pens provide quick voltage detection for safety verification before beginning electrical work.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools NCVT-2 Non-Contact Voltage Tester
Fluke 1AC-A1-II VoltAlert Non-Contact Voltage Tester

Installation and Wire Management

23. Wire Nuts and Connectors
Wire nuts and connectors are crucial for making secure electrical connections. Having a variety of sizes and types ensures you can handle different wire gauges and connection requirements safely and efficiently.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Ideal Industries Wire-Nut Connector
WAGO 221 Lever-Nut Connectors

24. Conduit Bender
A conduit bender is essential for shaping electrical conduit to navigate around obstacles and create professional-looking installations. Choose a bender appropriate for the conduit sizes you work with most frequently.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Greenlee 841FH Hand Bender
Klein Tools EMT Bender

25. Stud Finder
A reliable stud finder helps locate wall studs, metal pipes and electrical wiring behind walls. This prevents accidental damage when drilling or cutting and ensures secure mounting of electrical boxes and fixtures.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Zircon StudSensor e50 Electronic Wall Scanner
Franklin Sensors ProSensor 710 Precision Stud Finder

26. Wire Markers and Labels
Wire markers and labels help identify circuits and organize electrical installations for easier maintenance and troubleshooting.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Brady Wire Marker Book – Preprinted Numbers
Electrical Wire Labels – Self Adhesive

27. Wire Pulling Lubricant
Wire pulling lubricant reduces friction when pulling cables through conduit, making the job easier and preventing damage to wire insulation. It’s especially important for longer runs and multiple wire pulls.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Ideal Industries Yellow 77 Wire Pulling Lubricant
Klein Tools Wire Pulling Lubricant

28. Wire Pulling System
A magnetic wire pulling system makes running wires through walls significantly easier by turning a two-person job into a one-person operation. These systems use powerful magnets to guide wires through tight spaces and around obstacles.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools 50611 Magnetic Wire Puller
Klein Tools SRS56037 Magnetic Wire Pulling System

29. Wire Pulling Grips
Wire pulling grips provide a secure connection point for pulling cables through conduits and tight spaces. They distribute pulling force evenly to prevent damage to cable jackets.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools Wire Pulling Grips 1625-20
Greenlee Cable Pulling Grips

30. Fish Tape
When it comes to running wires through walls, ceilings or conduits, fish tape is one of the top must-have electricians tools. It is a long, flexible metal tape that allows you to navigate tight spaces and pull wires without damaging walls or insulation. Look for a fish tape with a comfortable grip and a smooth reeling mechanism.

Top Expert Recommendations 
Klein Tools 56005 Fish Tape
Greenlee 438-5 Steel Fish Tape

31. Electrical Tape
High-quality electrical tape is essential for insulating wire connections and protecting splices. Look for tape that’s rated for electrical use and provides excellent adhesion and flexibility. A variety of colors helps with wire identification and organization.

Top Expert Recommendations:
3M Scotch Super 33+ Electrical Tape
WarriorWrap General Tape

32. Cable Fault Locator
Cable fault locators help identify the exact location of faults in underground or buried cables, saving time and reducing excavation costs.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Megger Cable Fault Locators
Fluke T5-1000 Cable Fault Location Systems

33. Conduit Reamer
Conduit reamers remove burrs and sharp edges from cut conduit ends, protecting wire insulation during cable installation.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools 85191 Conduit Reaming Screwdriver
Conduit Deburring Tools – Various Options

34. Digital Caliper
Digital calipers provide precise measurements of wire gauges, conduit sizes and electrical component dimensions.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Neiko 01407A Electronic Digital Caliper
TACKLIFE Digital Caliper 6-Inch

35. Cable Pulling Winch
Cable pulling winches provide mechanical advantage for pulling heavy cables through long conduit runs and difficult installations.

Top Expert Recommendations:
VEVOR Electric Wire Puller 1100 LBS
NEIKO Portable Cable Pulling Winch

Power and Specialty Tools

36. Drill and Bits
A high-quality cordless drill with various bits is indispensable for creating holes in walls, electrical boxes and panels. Look for a drill with good battery life and torque control for different materials.

Top Expert Recommendations:
DEWALT DCD771C2 20V MAX Cordless Drill
Milwaukee M18 Fuel Hammer Drill

37. Pipe Threading Machine
Pipe threading machines cut precise threads on conduit and pipe for secure connections in electrical installations.

Top Expert Recommendations:
RIDGID 535 Threading Machines
VEVOR Pipe Threading Machine

38. Cable Tray Cutter
Cable tray cutters make clean, precise cuts in cable trays and raceways, ensuring proper fit and professional installations.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Greenlee Cable Tray Cutters
DEWALT 20V MAX XR Wire Cutter Kit

39. Torque Wrench
Torque wrenches ensure electrical connections are tightened to manufacturer specifications, preventing loose connections and overheating.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools 57005 Torque Wrench 30-250 Inch-Pound
Klein Tools 57060 Master Electrician Torque Set

40. Load Bank Tester
Load bank testers simulate electrical loads to test power systems, generators and UPS units under controlled conditions.

Top Expert Recommendations:
ELECOM NESTOUT Portable Rugged Power Bank 
400 Watt Electronic Load Tester

Digital Tools for the Modern Electrician

41. Electrical Panel Schedule Software
Digital tools for creating and managing electrical panel schedules help maintain accurate documentation of electrical systems.

Top Expert Recommendations:
AutoCAD Electrical Software
Breaker Panel Labels – Various Options

42. Cable Fault Finder
Cable fault finders locate breaks, shorts and other faults in cables using time domain reflectometry and other advanced techniques.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Cable Fault Locator TDR Tester
Network Cable Tester with TDR Function

43. Fiber Optic Test Equipment
Fiber optic test equipment verifies the performance and integrity of fiber optic cables used in modern communication systems.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fiber Optic Cleaning Kit
OTDR Fiber Optic Cable Tester

44. Power Quality Analyzer
Power quality analyzers monitor and analyze electrical power systems to identify problems like harmonics, voltage sags and power factor issues.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke 435-II Power Quality Analyzer
Fluke 435-II/BASIC Power Quality Analyzer

45. Ground Resistance Tester
Ground resistance testers measure the resistance of electrical grounding systems to ensure they meet safety standards and provide adequate protection.

Top Expert Recommendations:
EITAI200A-S Gen 2 Ground Resistance Tester
Extech 382152 Ground Resistance Tester

46. Phase Rotation Tester
Phase rotation testers determine the phase sequence of three-phase electrical systems, ensuring motors and equipment operate in the correct direction.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Extech Phase Sequence Tester
REED Instruments R5004 Motor Rotation and 3-Phase Tester

47. Motor Analyzer
Motor analyzers test and troubleshoot electric motors, helping identify issues like winding problems, rotor defects and insulation breakdown.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke 43B/003 Power Quality Analyzer
Extech 382100 1200A 3-Phase Power Analyzer/Datalogger

48. Hipot Tester
Hipot (high potential) testers apply high voltage to test the dielectric strength of insulation in electrical equipment and verify safety standards.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Associated Research Hipot Testers
Megger Dielectric Test Sets

49. Battery Tester
Battery testers analyze the condition and capacity of backup batteries in emergency lighting systems, UPS units and other critical applications.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke BT510 Battery Analyzer
Megger Digital/Analog Insulation Tester

50. Loop Impedance Tester
Loop impedance testers measure the impedance of electrical circuits to verify that protective devices will operate correctly during fault conditions.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Fluke 1672 Multifunction Installation Tester
CEM DT-6650 Multifunction Installation Tester

51. Continuity Tester
Continuity testers verify that electrical circuits are complete and identify breaks or high-resistance connections in wiring systems.

Top Expert Recommendations:
Klein Tools Continuity Tester
Fluke T5-1000 Voltage and Continuity Tester
